violations.text: A container of rice was observed adulterated by rodent activity (gnawing marks & pieces of plastic inside the product). Rice was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2026

Pasta, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2026

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2026

A dead mouse was observed in a trap in the basement at time of inspection. Dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests shall be removed from the premises at a frequency that presents their accumulation, decomposition or the attraction of pests. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of rodent droppings: 1) Dry storage shelving 2) Equipment storage shelving 3) Bar ledge Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2026
The floors throughout the facility have an accumulation of rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2026

Raw animal foods (liquid eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (bread, lettuce) in the reach-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (cutting board) were observed with an accumulation of rodent droppings. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher (bar & kitchen) was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Establishment to sanitize in 3 bay sink until serviced and dispensing proper concentration. Proof to be sent to inspector. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2026

Pasta, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2026

Pasta and sausage were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2026

A dead mouse was observed in a trap in the basement at time of inspection. Dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests shall be removed from the premises at a frequency that presents their accumulation, decomposition or the attraction of pests. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2026

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings throughout kitchen and basement. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dishwasher (bar & kitchen). New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of rodent droppings: 1) Dry storage shelving (basement) 2) Rolling rack 3) Sheet pan 4) Dishwasher (top) 5) Bar ledge (behind three bay sink) 6) Bar storage shelving New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2026
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2026

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the bar . Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025
The hand-washing sink in the bar was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the bar dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

Cheese was observed held at 53 degrees Fahrenheit in the prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, cheese was placed in the prep unit 2.5 hours prior to inspection. Cheese was relocated to the walk-in cooler to cool. Cheese was observed stored over the fill line. Establishment must not store food over the fill line. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025
Pasta salad was observed expired. Pasta salad must not be held past 7 days. Pasta was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

Chlorine sanitizer solution for the sanitizing bucket was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025
An insect control device (light trap) was observed installed above a cold-hold unit. Insect control devices must not be stored above food storage areas. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2025
Food was observed stored on the floor in the freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

The ice machine is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

At the time of inspection, the chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher at the Bar was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. (At the time of inspection, the Chlorine Sanitizer Chemical Bottle was not connected to the sanitizer tubing of the dish machine. Bottle connected at the time of inspection.) Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the Basement Walk-In Cooler Fan Cover was observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/26/2023
